description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages: test and coverage. Use a hidden template .test_images that sets the Docker image to $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/${IMAGE_NAMESPACE}:${TEST_IMAGE_TAG} and runs jobs in parallel for IMAGE_NAMESPACE values base and ruby-latest. In the test stage add a specs job that extends .test_images, .with-bundle, .test and .common-ci-tokens, runs git config --global user.email "you@example.com" and git config --global user.name "Your Name", then executes bundle exec rspec --format progress --format RspecJunitFormatter --out rspec.xml, and publishes coverage/assets, coverage/index.html and rspec.xml as artifacts with a JUnit report. Add a rubocop job extending .with-bundle, .test and .common-ci-tokens that runs bundle exec rubocop. Add a renovate-user-validate job extending .with-bundle, .test and .common-ci-tokens that runs ./scripts/generate-renovate-reviewers.sh and git diff --exit-code, and is allowed to fail. Add a go linter job extending .test-go and .common-ci-tokens that runs cd metrics, mise install go golangci-lint and golangci-lint run -v --color=always. Add a go test job extending .test-go and .common-ci-tokens that runs cd metrics, mise install go and go test gitlab.com/gitlab-org/release-tools/metrics/.... Add a delivery-metrics acceptance tests job extending .test, .ci-image-com and .common-ci-tokens that runs ./scripts/delivery-metrics.sh build, then ./scripts/delivery-metrics.sh run & , waits for a Docker container named delivery_metrics to appear, and finally runs ./scripts/delivery-metrics.sh acceptance-tests, with a timeout of 30m and a variable RUNNER_SCRIPT_TIMEOUT set to 15m. In the coverage stage add a pages job that depends on specs, runs only on push to the default branch (and never when CI_SERVER_HOST is "ops.gitlab.net"), creates a public/ directory, moves coverage/ into public/, and publishes the public/ directory as an artifact.
